label: Intellectual Property Rights
description: Users must provide clear and accurate attribution when using, sharing, or modifying the intellectual property. Attribution includes identifying the original creator and the source of the work.

label: Intellectual Property Rights
description: Users must respect the moral rights of the creator, ensuring that the work is not altered in a way that could harm the creator’s reputation or integrity.
Data controllers must obtain clear, informed, and unambiguous consent from the data subject before collecting or processing personal data.
Data controllers are obligated to implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure the security of personal data, protecting it from unauthorized access, breaches, or misuse.
Users of the work must provide clear attribution to the creator, acknowledging their contribution and ensuring the work is credited properly.
Users must respect the moral integrity of the creator’s work and refrain from actions that could distort or misrepresent the original content or intent.
Users must provide proper attribution to the data owner when using, sharing, or distributing the data.
Users must ensure that they follow the terms of the license under which the data is provided.
Researchers must provide proper attribution to the original authors and sources when using prior work in their research.
Researchers are required to publish their outputs in open-access repositories when mandated by their funding bodies or institutions.
Users must ensure that license information is prominently displayed along with the content, enabling others to understand the terms of use.
Users are obligated to align their actions with the terms and conditions specified in the applied license.
Users must provide proper attribution to the original source when accessing or reusing content, ensuring credit is given to the creators or owners.
Users must comply with the license terms under which the content is made available, ensuring responsible and lawful use.
Researchers are obligated to ensure that their data and results are accurate, reliable, and reproducible to maintain the credibility of their research.
Researchers must adhere to established ethical standards and institutional policies governing their work.
Controllers must inform individuals about how their data is collected, processed, and stored, including the purpose of processing and their rights.
Controllers are required to implement appropriate security measures to protect personal data from breaches or unauthorized access.
Researchers are obligated to deposit their research outputs in recognized open access repositories to ensure accessibility and compliance with funding requirements.

Rights holders are responsible for monitoring the use of their intellectual property to identify and address potential infringements.
All team members must acknowledge the contributions of collaborators, ensuring equitable recognition of individual efforts.
Teams are obligated to clearly define roles and responsibilities to ensure fair participation and accountability among collaborators.

Data providers are responsible for implementing appropriate measures to protect sensitive data and ensuring its responsible use.

The data controller must guarantee that consent has been validly obtained, recorded, and stored.
The platform must verify that the connector’s declared security profile matches the required level before granting access.
Requesters must declare their intended purpose and log it before gaining access to the dataset.
The platform must validate that the requester holds the role of Researcher before allowing access.
The requester must submit a valid ethics approval certificate before accessing the dataset.
The data processor must document and audit the technical measures applied before data is accessed.
The data platform must monitor the data volume per request and enforce the limit automatically.
The system must verify the physical or jurisdictional location of the requester before allowing access.
The platform must verify the authenticity of the declared identity through an approved TSP (e.g., eIDAS-compliant IdP).
The platform must check the revocation status of the data before granting access.
Notify the audit endpoint upon each access request to ensure accountability.
The platform must monitor and record the number of accesses to enforce this constraint.
Verify the requester’s IP-derived or declared location before access is granted.
Ensure the dataset is removed after the allowed time window to comply with data lifecycle obligations.
The platform must ensure that the requester's declared security profile matches the expected threshold.
A notification about data access must be sent to a trusted logging or monitoring service.
Ensure the identity of the connector matches the expected URI prior to granting access.
The system must keep track of how many times the dataset has been accessed.
Track when access starts to ensure duration is not exceeded.
Validate the request timestamp against the allowed access interval.
Each access must be logged to the designated clearing house for traceability.
Send a notification with usage metadata (user, resource, timestamp) to the specified endpoint.
The platform must monitor and enforce the allowed number of uses per user or client.
The system must automatically revoke access after the specified duration.
The platform must evaluate and apply the defined access window.
The data must be deleted on 1 February 2025.
